relations.summary: Both create access to structure, but copy duplicates presentation while reference links indirectly.
Both reduce available structure, but projection selects while collapse compresses.
Embedding places structure within containment; binding attaches dependency or constraint.
Split and merge move in opposing structural directions.
Collapse reduces structure; expand exposes or elaborates structure.
Bind and unbind move in opposing dependency directions.
Both define successor structure, but versioning is sequential while branching is divergent.
Authorization concerns permission; attestation concerns evidence.
Shift relocates across frame; reorder rearranges internal order.
Reference links indirectly; embedding places structure inside a containing structure.
